codeReview.summary: The commit improves RecoverSuccess copy/reveal/clear behavior against VAL-REC-002/003, but the implementation does not satisfy the feature contract for a 3-step recover flow (collect -> reveal -> success). Required reveal route/scenario and success-completion content are missing, and collect-screen intake content does not match the feature's expected behavior.

issues.description: Feature expects a dedicated `/recover/{profileId}/reveal` step, but routes only register `/recover/:profileId` and `/recover/:profileId/success`; there is no reveal route.

Feature preconditions/expected behavior require `recover-reveal-nsec`, but demo scenarios only include `recover-collect-shares` and `recover-success`, so the required reveal screen is not testable from DemoGallery.

The `/recover/:profileId/success` screen renders the reveal/security-warning UI instead of the expected success confirmation state (`NSEC Recovered` green card + `Return to Dashboard` CTA), so the feature's success-step requirements are not implemented.

Collect Shares intake does not implement the expected password fields and threshold-progress section (it currently renders only a paste-share text input and a `Recover NSEC` CTA), so `/recover/{profileId}` is not at the specified content parity.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission artifacts conflict on Recover flow shape: feature metadata expects 3 screens (`collect`, `reveal`, `success`) while validation contract and Paper references cover only 2 (`collect`, `success`). This led to a worker handoff that marked success while explicitly omitting `/reveal`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json:1097-1114 expects `/recover/{profileId}/reveal` + `recover-reveal-nsec`; AGENTS.md:15 says 'Collect Shares / Reveal NSEC / Success'; validation-contract.md:330-707 defines only VAL-REC-001..003 for collect/success; igloo-paper/screens/recover contains only `1-collect-shares` and `1b-recover-success`; handoff file line 67 states `/reveal` was intentionally not added.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-builder skill strongly prioritizes validation-contract/Paper parity, but does not enforce escalation when feature expectedBehavior conflicts with those sources; in this case the worker followed contract-only scope and completed without feature-level parity.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/ui-builder/SKILL.md:18 says expected copy comes from validation contract; handoff line 67 documents omission of `/reveal` due Paper/contract mismatch while feature metadata still required it.

summary: Review failed: recover-fidelity commit is internally consistent with current VAL-REC checks, but it does not meet the feature contract requiring a dedicated reveal step and success-completion screen/content parity.
